personal.name: Aarju Pal
personal.summary: Backend Software Engineer specialising in AI-driven systems and backend infrastructure, with production internship experience at PayPal and Rippling. At PayPal, built a LangGraph multi-agent system with Milvus that reduced ticket creation time by 92%; at Rippling, built backend frameworks and tooling now serving 560,000+ page saves/month across 78 countries.

taglines: If you're still here, just email me already.
Mostly backend. Sometimes AI. Always shipping.
Stitching backends with a bit of AI glue.

bullets: Migrated 183 employee data fields across 20+ countries from a legacy multi-class system to an internal define-once, use-everywhere framework; fields now serve 560,000+ page saves/month on systems powering $114.25M in EOR ARR across 78 countries.

Owned two complexity-5 migrations in the entire project (FLSA Exempt, Company Employment Type); redesigned the standard approach into an 8-PR incremental plan when the original PR grew to 10,000+ lines — zero margin for error on a US regulatory compliance field.
Co-built Conditionality Engine during Quality Week, won Foundational Fixes category at the company-wide event.
Designed an AI-driven Change Management PoC using Python, LangGraph, and Milvus, reducing ticket creation time by 90%.
Built backend automation tools and MCP integrations, reducing developer friction by 8,000 hours/month.
Developed and shipped Java Spring Boot REST APIs for backend services across QA and production environments as part of the full SDLC.

projects.name: Conditionality Engine
bullets: Built a declarative rules engine on Django REST that replaces hand-written Python lambda overrides with database-stored rules, reducing field behavior changes from a full engineering cycle to 5 minutes via a UI, enabling self-serve rule management by non-engineers.
Analysis of 2,300+ production overrides showed 1,200 (50%+) directly migrateable; projected 70-80% coverage with regex and feature flag support.
Engine inserted at priority 3 in the middleware chain — zero-risk incremental adoption per field.

projects.name: AI-Driven Change Management Automation
bullets: Designed and built an internal AI-driven backend system using Python to automate creation, validation, and enrichment of change management tickets.
Implemented a LangGraph-based multi-agent system integrated with Milvus vector database to enable semantic search, context retrieval, and intelligent workflow orchestration.
Reduced average ticket creation time from 1 hour to 5 minutes (92% reduction), significantly improving engineering productivity and operational efficiency.
